subtopic: ISFR 2023 and UN High Seas Treaty
title: ISFR Data and Forest Key Terms
focus: State-wise forest cover rankings, mangrove cover changes, NDC carbon stock targets, and definitions of key forest-related terms.
rationale: This section contains highly testable factual data regarding India's forest cover rankings and precise definitions of forest area versus forest cover, which are classic UPSC prelims traps.
sectionExcerpt: Details the top states in forest cover by area (Madhya Pradesh, Arunachal Pradesh, Chhattisgarh) and percentage (Lakshadweep, Mizoram, A&N Islands). Notes a decrease in total mangrove cover to 4,992 sq km. Highlights India's NDC target progress in carbon stock. Defines key terms including Forest Area, Forest Cover (>= 1 hectare, >= 10% canopy), Tree Cover, Carbon Stock, and Protected Forests.
evidenceBullets: Top 3 states by forest area: Madhya Pradesh, Arunachal Pradesh, Chhattisgarh.
Top 3 states by forest percentage: Lakshadweep (91.33%), Mizoram, Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
Total Mangrove cover decreased by 7.43 sq km to 4,992 sq km (Gujarat decreased; AP and Maharashtra increased).
Forest Cover requires >= 1 hectare area and >= 10% tree canopy, irrespective of ownership.
Tree cover comprises patches < 1 hectare outside recorded forest areas.

subtopic: ISFR 2023 and UN High Seas Treaty
title: UN High Seas Treaty (BBNJ) and UNCLOS
focus: Provisions of the BBNJ Agreement, its relationship with UNCLOS, and the legal boundaries of maritime zones.
rationale: International treaties and maritime boundaries are high-yield for UPSC. The BBNJ agreement is a major recent development, and UNCLOS maritime zones (EEZ, Territorial Sea) are fundamental geography and IR concepts.
sectionExcerpt: Covers the BBNJ Agreement (High Seas Treaty), a legally binding treaty under UNCLOS aimed at conserving marine biodiversity beyond national jurisdiction. It outlines key pillars like Marine Protected Areas (MPAs), Marine Genetic Resources (MGRs), and Environmental Impact Assessments. Also details UNCLOS maritime zones: Territorial Sea (12 nm), Contiguous Zone (24 nm), and EEZ (200 nm), along with dispute settlement mechanisms like ITLOS.
evidenceBullets: BBNJ is the third implementation agreement under UNCLOS (others: 1994 Part XI and 1995 Fish Stocks).
BBNJ requires 60 ratifications to enter into force; India has signed but not ratified.
Non-UNCLOS members can also join the BBNJ agreement.
UNCLOS Maritime Zones: Territorial Sea (up to 12 nm, full sovereignty), Contiguous Zone (up to 24 nm), EEZ (up to 200 nm).
Deep Seabed beyond national jurisdiction is the 'common heritage of mankind', managed by the International Seabed Authority (ISA).

subtopic: ISFR 2023 and UN High Seas Treaty
title: Ramsar Convention on Wetlands
focus: Overview of the Ramsar Convention, its mandate, and recent CoP15 details.
rationale: Ramsar is a staple environmental topic for UPSC. The recent CoP15 and the definition of 'wise use' are key examinable points.
sectionExcerpt: Summarizes the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands following its 15th Conference of Parties (CoP15) in Zimbabwe. It defines the 'wise use' of wetlands, notes its genesis in 1971 (entered into force in 1975), and highlights its 172 member countries (India joined in 1982) with a secretariat in Gland, Switzerland.
evidenceBullets: Ramsar CoP15 was held in Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe with the theme 'Protecting Wetlands for Our Common Future'.
'Wise use' means maintaining ecological character using ecosystem-based approaches within sustainable development.
Adopted in 1971 in Ramsar, Iran; entered into force in 1975; India joined in 1982.
Secretariat is located in Gland, Switzerland.
